item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as the image comes from here and is All Rights Reserved. Thus, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Huh. It's awfully long given the amount of info and story we get. It could do with a bit of compression.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The fact that they are capable of this makes the reason why to date only three (3) have attempted to escape the building unclear.

This sentence really needs to be untangled. "Makes it unclear why only three have attempted to escape the building to date", or something

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That audio file left the distinct impression of a Nazgul. So now I can easily imagine a female Ringwraith pulling the strings of the entire scip.
Otherwise, I like the horror and effects of the logs even though they're shorter than the one exploration log of 2951 or anything from 1730 or 093. Very concise yet chilling. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While I was annoyed by it, I assumed that the log itself was infohazardous because the containment pricedure references it being redacted specifically.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Send a Class D with a ingot of gold into the building. Next step, send a drone (fast, with capacity to carry several kilogram of weight) to recover the "cloned ingots". After the recovery the Foundation can sell the "cloned gold". Profit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would like to add that the third log being redacted *kills* the SCP instead of hurting it. "It was so spooky I couldn't describe it!" is a trope that should've ended with Lovecraft.

item.title: 3rd Log Redaction
item.content:encoded:
The one thing I have to complain about is the 3rd log being redacted. In my opinion, it's unnecessary and hurts the SCP overall.

Everything else is good, in my opinion. +1

item.title: 3rd Expedition
item.content:encoded:
"You do not have permission to view this file"

Oh fuck you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Two things annoyed me about this.

The new primary objective of all efforts concerning the SCP is the identification, retrieval, and containment of said being. In confronting her, researchers are instructed to [DATA EXPUNGED].

"In confronting her, researchers are instructed to dance a jolly jig."

And then there's the third log, which is built up throughout the entire article, being redacted. That's like giving someone an ice cream cone and not telling them there's a dead fly at the bottom.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really not getting why this one is so popular. It reminds me vaguely of something off Silent Hill or some horror point and click adventure, only that not as interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I felt like I've read this one a few times, and the third file is just annoying. Not cute, or funny, or thought provoking. -vote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i'm left wanting by the third exploration, but upvote for the second

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm a sucker for exploration logs, mostly if said logs involve enclosed locations in which weird stuff happens. This one though, did nothing for me. The information given before the logs tells you everything that is going to happen in the logs, which makes them redundant to read since you gain nothing new from them. The logs themselves were also a pain to get through, since the dialogue was too exaggerated and the descriptions weren't anything special.

What bothers me most, though, is the bad use of expungements.

In confronting her, researchers are instructed to [DATA EXPUNGED].

If they encounter her (which should be an it btw, since its an anomaly) what are they supposed to do? Guess? Shout "[DATA EXPUNGED]"? Restricting vital information from researchers will just end in trouble. It is also really lazy, since there are absolutely no hints on what it is, so for all I know they are supposed to do cow noises until the anomaly leaves them alone.
The lack of a third log is even worse though. Yeah, it was cute when SCP-087 did it, but there it was handled well, with the previews logs actually being interesting and building suspense as each progressed. In here, the first log just shows you what was already told, and the second one just adds a bit to it. Then there's nothing. This lacks good pacing and instead of making me come up with all the horrible things that could have happened, it just makes me feel like you couldn't bother to write a third log and just slapped an expungement to it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Never upvoted this, and now I know why.

Your image is hosted off-site. Please save it to the page. This is a rule.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What's with the third transcript?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm. It's a ruined building which makes oh-so-spooky clones of you which then try to destroy the building, and there's something else in there which is not explained at all. And then there's a sound clip of some static and slowed-down speech. It doesn't impress me.
Great job. I absolutely love the atmosphere.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It is a pun of a "Youth Hostel", a place of cheap lodging often used by twenty-something backpackers on their post-college/but before they enter the workforce vacation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Youth Hostiles"?
I don't get it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The access restriction of the third expedition log is part of the containment procedure? To me, that implies there's an infohazardous aspect to the object…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Disappointed that the 3rd log is classified.

It is like a great story without an ending.
We know that the task force gets sent in in the third log, possibly heavily armed and armored, and gets slaughtered. Writing the log for that wouldn't reveal too much information about the SCP, as if it did, the SCP would be explained in universe. It currently isn't, they still don't know what is going on according to the rest of the article, so nothing in the 3rd log would reveal too much about it.

That is unless the in universe author, and the other researchers are really slow.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Goddamnit author, reveal the third log!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Never saw this one before. Very 087-esque. I love this, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Somehow I never upvoted this. Rectifying that now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is pretty damn great. Personally, the amount of information given about what happened in Log 003 is just enough to where leaving it out feels more like a disappointment than a 087-style scare, but the rest is good enough so that I upvote regardless. Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Her, that whats gets my upvote. When confronting HER. I'm imagining the bitch from Coraline but 10x more terrifying.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Creepy, but the lack of explanation of the last paragraph of the description irks me. I understand why it's there, but the problem is that this lacks a central idea to tie this together without it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like there are some minor problems in this holding the whole thing back. Odd turns of phrase, like "massively decrepit", that feel out of tone, not to mention the whole intro to the second video log. The premise is definitely good though, as is the picture.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Goddamn, that soundfile makes me want to upvote this one again…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Motherfucker. Building up to that third log, only to have it not exist… Dammit!

That's how much I love this article really. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is really very good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just the type of article I like. Though I was sad to see that there wasn't a third log.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You don't know how disappointed I was to open the third log and find it nonexistent. Still good nonetheless.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was ready to upvote at "Youth Hostiles", one of the better MTF puns I've seen in a while. Including mine.

Then the rest was just…. Deliciously creepy, with a bit of the good old "Holy shit run for your life"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The fact that they are capable of this makes the reason why to date only three (3) have attempted to escape the building unclear.

Instead write, "However, only three have made their way out of the building." or something to that effect. Good job though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love MTF names.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Sir, where should the D-class put the nuclear warhead?"

"What warhead? OH GOD!"

BOOM

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, although I think the logs could be tightened up a bit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: it's really bothering me that the logs say nothing about dealing with the major tilt. sure, it says it didn't happen till expedition 003, but it's a shame to not take advantage of it at all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: YOUTH HOSTILES +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dommot and it worked just when I got back to this!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yup, that's just you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is it just me or is the image broken?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Man screw that sound file. Have my +1 you jerk….. <3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted.

But, I do agree with Crayne in some aspects; the language is a bit 'iffy' on occasions. Nonetheless, it was creepy, and interesting in what it was.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoting, but you need to reread this and tighten up the language. Things like "As a whole, SCP-1919-1 exhibits a swarm intelligence akin to that typical of an ant colony." sound convoluted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This just plays on so many of my little phobias. I'm a bit frightened to open the sound file, though, primarily because of the use of the phrase 'headphones recommended'. Still, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like I just watched an episode on the Gangers in Doctor Who.

the sound file though…+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh god thats creepy. and that sound file…. I've got goosebumps all over. upvoted for sure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think I remember seeing this in your sandbox! Read and upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Many will come later to offer detailed feedback. I, for my part, am tired and incoherent at the moment. So I'll just leave you with, "fuck. yes."

+1
